free_text: Activation cross-sections of longer lived products of
deuteron induced nuclear reactions on ytterbium up to
40 MeV
Elemental cross-sections on natYb targets were
measured using the activation method and the standard
stacked foil irradiation technique combined with high-
resolution gamma-ray spectrometry.
The irradiated stack had a complex structure,
consisting of a nine times repeated sequence of
Rh (12.3 um), Ho(25 um), Au(10.7 um), Yb(23 um),
MoRe alloy (50 um), CuMnNi alloy (25 um) foils and the
Al(100 um) foils serving as monitor.
The irradiation of the targets was performed in He-gas
atmosphere in a water cooled target holder.
The activity of each sample was assessed
nondestructively in two series of measurements.
The decay data were taken from NUDAT 2.6.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty
of incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping
power of each foil due to thickness variation and
straggling effects.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the standard way following the
recommendations of the ISO-guide as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters.
Uncertainty of time parameters is not included.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
Entry was checked by the corresponding author.
